#  Edit Drawing Markups (iOS) 
##### Tip
A new [two and three-column layout](https://v2.support.procore.com/faq-what-layouts-are-available-for-ipad) is now available for iPads, making it easier to navigate different tools. Tap the **menu** ![Icon Ipad Menu](https://images.ctfassets.net/plvbd9i6qkpm/2wyHOqBLAvCO7klaHLXZzj/5f9e5da4183e99ab1ef8a7ee2530d1b0/icon_ipad_menu.png?fm=webp&h=24) icon to show and hide the tools menu.
##  Things to Consider 
  * **Required User Permissions:**
    * _To edit any markups on both the 'Personal' and 'Published' layers,_ 'Admin' permissions on the project's Drawings tool.  
_Note_ : 'Standard' users can only edit personal and published markups that they have created.
    * Published markups can only be deleted by users with ‘Admin’ permissions on the Drawings tool, unless a user is [granted specific permissions to delete published markups](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-project/tutorials/allow-users-to-delete-published-markups).
  * **Additional Information:**
    * Any changes made to the markups will automatically be updated on the drawing's related layer.


##  Steps 
  1. Open the **Procore** app on an iOS mobile device.
  2. Tap the project's **Drawings** tool.
  3. Tap the drawing you want to edit markups on.
  4. Tap the markup you want to edit.
  5. While the markup is selected, you can perform the following actions:
     * **Link items** : Tap **Link** and select the item that you want to link to the markup. See [Link Items to a Drawing (iOS)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-ios/tutorials/link-items-to-a-drawing-ios).  
_Note:_ This option is not available for all item types.
     * **Copy and paste the markup** :  
_Note:_ You can copy and paste the following markup types: Pen, Highlight, Cloud, Box, Oval, Arrow, Line, Double Arrow, and Text.
       1. Tap **Copy** on the markup.
       2. Move the markup to the area of the drawing that you want to add it to.
       3. Tap **Paste**.
     * **Resize the markup** : Drag the corners of the markup to adjust the size and shape.
     * **Move the markup** : Drag the markup to a different area of the drawing.
     * **Delete the markup** : Tap **Delete**. See [Delete Drawing Markups (iOS)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-ios/tutorials/delete-drawing-markups-ios).


## See Also

  * [Mark Up a Drawing (iOS)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-ios/tutorials/mark-up-a-drawing-ios)
  * [View Drawings (iOS)](https://v2.support.procore.com/product-manuals/drawings-ios/tutorials/view-drawings-ios)
title: Edit Drawing Markups (iOS)
description: How to edit drawing markups on the Procore iOS app.
